Australia’s Jindalee Operational Radar Network is set to help Canada build the biggest and most advanced over-the-horizon radar…...

Australia's proven JORN technology transfer to Canada demonstrates how allied nations are consolidating radar capabilities to monitor vast Arctic airspace cost-effectively against emerging threats.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

Manna expands drone delivery network with $50M boost

Ireland-based drone delivery company Manna Air Delivery is making a serious push into the US, and it just secured the funding to back it up. The company announced a fresh $50 million Series B round, bringing its total funding to $110 million, as it prepares to rapidly scale operations across the...

Manna's $50M Series B positions autonomous delivery as a competitive logistics alternative, signaling investor confidence in regulatory pathways for scaled drone operations.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation from accident investigation to systematic hazard identification, reducing incidents before they occur rather than analyzing them afterward.

Delta's Massive Growth Continues: Record Flights On 3 New Long-Haul Routes

Delta's transatlantic expansion addresses rising demand for premium long-haul connectivity, positioning the carrier to capture higher-margin international revenue amid post-pandemic leisure travel recovery.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

DJI confirms end-of-support timeline for Mavic 2, Matrice 600 drones

Tech giant DJI will officially end service and support for several of its most beloved professional and prosumer drones in the coming weeks. That includes the iconic DJI Mavic 2 Pro, the versatile DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Zoom and DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Dual, and the heavy-lift powerhouse DJI Matrice...

DJI's end-of-support timeline for legacy professional drones signals a broader industry shift toward forcing upgrades to newer platforms with enhanced capabilities and connectivity requirements.

Lumberjack's autonomous capabilities enable distributed operations across contested environments, reducing pilot workload and extending battlefield persistence beyond traditional UAS constraints.

Iran War Fallout: Airlines Slash Thousands Of Flights Amid Global Fuel Shortages

As the Iran crisis escalates, airlines worldwide are grappling with skyrocketing fuel costs and limited access to jet fuel.

Geopolitical disruptions are forcing capacity reductions across global networks as hedging costs and supply constraints compress already-thin fuel margins.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading fatal helicopter hazard because pilots systematically overestimate their reaction time and wire visibility in flight conditions.

Always trying to resolve issues before they start.

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

Drought Parches Florida

The state was unusually dry for much of 2025, but the intensity of the drought has ratcheted up since January 2026.

Extended drought conditions in Florida may strain water resources critical for aircraft de-icing, cooling systems, and firefighting capabilities at major aviation hubs.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Artemis 2 swings around the moon

Four astronauts from the United States and Canada became the humans to travel the furthest from the Earth April 6 as they went around the moon on the Artemis 2 mission.

Artemis 2's successful lunar flyby validates NASA's SLS and Orion systems for crewed deep-space missions, clearing the path for lunar landing operations.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

The Problem With Ultra Long-Haul Flights Airlines Don’t Talk About

Why Air New Zealand is unable to compete on its flagship route.

Crew fatigue and duty-time regulations on ultra long-haul routes create operational constraints that economics alone cannot overcome, limiting competitive viability for smaller carriers.

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as critical training hubs where flight schools develop pilot talent while simultaneously handling commercial air service, creating a dual-purpose economic model.

Air Canada Expands To The Canary Islands & Latin America, Thanks To The Airbus A321XLR & A220

Air Canada's ultra-long-range A321XLR deployment enables profitable transatlantic routes to leisure destinations previously requiring connecting flights or larger aircraft.

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Here's How Air Canada's Airbus A321XLR 1-1 Lie-Flat Suites Compare To Widebody Business Class

The A321XLR's 4,700-nautical-mile range opens secondary European cities but does the 12.2-foot-wide cabin match the widebody experience?

The A321XLR's narrow-body economics enable profitable routes to secondary European markets, but the 12.2-foot cabin width creates a fundamentally different premium passenger experience than traditional widebody business class.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

“There’s A Huge Demand”: The Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner Demolished After Only 13 Flying Hours

A barely flown Boeing 787-8 is scrapped because its parts are worth more than the whole.

Aircraft parting-out economics signal potential oversupply in used widebody inventory, creating arbitrage opportunities that challenge traditional aircraft lifecycle assumptions.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through technology integration offers airlines and airports a near-term pathway to accommodate growing demand while reducing environmental impact without requiring aircraft fleet replacement.

See MDPI →

China has completed the maiden flight of a new 7-tonne autonomous cargo aircraft, taking a...

NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight

Artemis II's lunar trajectory achievements demonstrate advanced spacecraft navigation capabilities that will inform next-generation deep-space exploration vehicle design and operational parameters.

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview

NASA’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24, will deliver approximately 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. This mission will be the second flight of the Cygnus XL, the larger, more cargo-capable version of the...

Cygnus XL's expanded capacity represents a significant leap in ISS resupply efficiency, potentially reducing mission frequency and operational costs for sustained station operations.

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

K-State Salina Launches High School Aviation Camp

Weeklong program will include simulator work, ground school and up to three hours of flight time.

Hands-on aviation camps combining simulator training with actual flight experience are expanding pipeline development efforts to younger demographics before college enrollment.

Moog's METEOR satellite bus platform positions the company to capture growing demand from commercial space operators seeking standardized, modular spacecraft architecture.

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

FOSSA targets Japan’s defense market as larger smallsats expand capabilities

Spanish startup FOSSA Systems is pushing into Japan’s defense market after securing a local partner to expand its reach, building on a shift beyond tiny picosatellites used to connect low-power monitoring devices toward more capable spacecraft for broader government applications.

FOSSA's pivot toward larger, more capable satellites signals a maturing small-sat market where defense budgets increasingly value operational flexibility over minimal cost, reshaping competitive dynamics in government space contracts.

Air Tractor Holdings Acquires Thrush Aircraft

Deal combines two ag aviation manufacturers under shared ownership.

The consolidation of two major agricultural aircraft manufacturers reduces competitive fragmentation in a niche market already facing pilot shortages and rising certification costs.

Hartzell Expands Top Prop Program

Update adds 150+ models, pricing changes for GA aircraft upgrades.

As Falcon Field and Mesa Gateway add landing fees for light aircraft, officials and flight schools describe different futures for the Phoenix area.

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II

Today, the crew of Artemis II reaches a milestone, traveling farther than any humans in recorded history, as they orbit the far side of the Moon, roughly 4,000 miles above the lunar surface. You can watch this historic broadcast on NASA’s YouTube channel today, April 6, 2026, at 10 AM Pacific (1 PM...

Artemis II's lunar orbit achievement demonstrates NASA's sustained capability to execute complex deep-space missions, validating the pathway for sustained human Moon exploration and eventual Mars missions.
